---
title: "Getting Started with Quarto"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Official Documentation
The official Quarto documentation at **[quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)** contains detailed tutorials for all skill levels — always a great place to look for more detail.
:::

Welcome! This guide walks you through creating real Quarto documents — step by step, like a building manual.
No prior experience needed.

---

## What would you like to build?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### 🌐 A Website
Present your course, research, or portfolio as a clean multi-page website.

→ [What you need](beg_website_1.qmd) 
→ [Create a website](beg_website_2.qmd)  
→ [Publish online](beg_website_3.qmd)  
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### 📄 An Exercise Sheet
Create a professional exercise sheet that renders to PDF, HTML, or Word from one file.

→ [What you need](beg_exercise_1.qmd)  
→ [Create a sheet](beg_exercise_2.qmd)  
→ [Other formats](beg_exercise_3.qmd)  
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### 📚 A Book
Structure longer content — lecture notes, a handbook — as a navigable book.

→ [What you need](beg_book_1.qmd)  
→ [Create a book](beg_book_2.qmd)  
:::

:::

---

## ✨ Want interactive Python code?

If you want students to run and edit Python code directly in the browser (no installation needed for them), check out the [Extensions guide](beg_extensions.qmd).
